title: Malty American Cardamom Pie
description: A sweet, malty pie with a glassy-jelly texture and a hint of cardamom, inspired by classic American desserts.

ingredients.name: Graham Cracker Crust
ingredients.notes: Use a store-bought crust or make your own with 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs and 1/4 cup sugar.

ingredients.name: Caramel Syrup
ingredients.notes: Make your own caramel syrup by melting 1 cup sugar and 1/2 cup heavy cream over low heat, stirring constantly.

ingredients.name: Ground Cardamom
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground cardamom for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Brown Sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a nice depth of flavor.

instructions: Roll out the graham cracker crust and press it into a 9-inch pie dish.
Bake the crust for 8-10 minutes, or until lightly browned.
In a medium saucepan, combine the caramel syrup, heavy cream, and granulated sugar. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, vanilla extract, and ground cardamom.
Pour the egg mixture into the saucepan with the caramel syrup and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into the baked pie crust and smooth the top.
If using brown sugar, sprinkle it evenly over the top of the pie.
Bake the pie for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is slightly jiggly.
Let the pie cool to room temperature before serving.
